text: <par>Gazhuo <latinform>-u</latinform> has been completely swallowed up by the nasal initial in <latinform>ŋ³³</latinform>, a phenomenon which is typical of Loloish: e.g. the Lahu phonemic syllable /<latinform>mu</latinform>/ is pronounced as a syllabic labiodental nasal [<plainlatinform>ɱ̩</plainlatinform>] (see Matisoff 1973, <emph>The Grammar of Lahu</emph>, pp. 3-4).</par>
<par>Is this tag right? Cf. FACE. 2nd morpheme homophonous with EYE?</par>
<par>fe:see 3 next p;fu below.=*<latinform>wu</latinform>? Rel. to <emph>pu</emph>? Why <emph>?</emph>?</par>
<par>Possible loan from Chinese, cf. Chinese gān 肝 'liver'.</par>
<par>Gazhuo <latinform>pao³³</latinform> evidently a loan from Chinese (cf. Mandarin <hanform>胞</hanform> <plainlatinform>bāo</plainlatinform>).</par>
